# Cook Web Domain: hooks

This file adds local guidance for `src/cook-web/src/hooks`. Read the
repository root and `src/cook-web/AGENTS.md` first, then use this file for
domain-specific frontend ownership.

## Scope

- This domain owns shared React hooks for auth, audio coordination, and other
  reusable frontend behavior.

- Representative files in this subtree: `src/cook-web/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`,
  `src/cook-web/src/hooks/useExclusiveAudio.ts`,
  `src/cook-web/src/hooks/useGoogleAuth.ts`,
  `src/cook-web/src/hooks/useToast.tsx`

- Keep durable frontend rules here only when they are more specific than the
  Cook Web-wide guidance in the parent directory.

- Use focused frontend verification around `src/cook-web/src/hooks/` before
  widening to global type and lint checks.

## Do

- Start from the local entry files and preserve how `hooks` currently
  separates route code, shared logic, state, and utilities.

- Keep hook inputs and returned fields stable, and update all consumers in the
  same task when the contract changes.

- Preserve browser and server assumptions explicitly so hooks do not
  accidentally run client-only code on the server.

- Treat hooks as reusable orchestration layers that should call shared stores
  and libs instead of duplicating low-level logic.

## Avoid

- Do not leave partially renamed hook return fields in consumers after a
  contract refactor.

- Do not hide API calls or state transitions in hooks without keeping the
  underlying shared utilities testable.

- Do not couple hooks to one page or one route if the behavior should stay
  generally reusable.

- Avoid pushing domain-local rules up to shared docs unless another frontend
  subtree truly depends on the same constraint.

## Commands

- `rg -n "hooks" src/cook-web/src` to locate call sites before editing this
  subtree.

- `cd src/cook-web && npm run type-check` after TypeScript, store, hook,
  request, or route-contract changes.

- `cd src/cook-web && npm run lint` before closing the task when shared
  frontend code or route files changed.

- `cd src/cook-web && npm run test -- src/hooks/` for focused Jest coverage
  when this subtree already has or gains tests.

## Tests

- Add or update focused tests closest to `src/cook-web/src/hooks/` for the
  happy path and the most important regression or business-code error path.

- Rerun type-checking and linting when exported props, store fields, hook
  contracts, or route behavior move.

- When a domain depends on shared request or normalization helpers, update
  those tests or add new ones in the same task.

- Document skipped UI, browser, or provider-dependent verification in the task
  summary when local automation cannot cover it.
